member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions
member.summary:
Ready-made conditions for . All of them read the payload Twitch Colony passes at draw time, so none of them touch the game — which is what keeps this library safe to load when Twitch Colony isn't installed.
Write your own the same way: ctx => whatever. They're checked every time the options are drawn, so keep them cheap and don't change anything from inside one.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions.FromCycle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Only from this colony cycle onwards. Good for events that would end a young colony.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions.BeforeCycle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Only before this colony cycle.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions.All(System.Func{System.Object,System.Boolean}[])
member.summary: All of them must say yes. No conditions = always yes.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions.Any(System.Func{System.Object,System.Boolean}[])
member.summary: Any one of them saying yes is enough. No conditions = always yes.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventConditions.Not(System.Func{System.Object,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Inverts a condition.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo
member.summary:
A snapshot of a registered event's state, handed back by . Everything you need to roll your own weighted choice over a set of events and then trigger the winner.
This type lives only in the merge-lib — it never crosses into Twitch Colony. The mod hands the values over as a plain dictionary and this side reads them into the typed shape below, which is why an add-on's own struct is fine here where it wouldn't be on the boundary.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.Id
member.summary: The event's stable id.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.DisplayName
member.summary: What chat sees in the vote options.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.GroupId
member.summary: The event's group id, or null if it stands on its own.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.Weight
member.summary: The event's configured draw weight (see , though a mod may use any value). 0 means it's never offered by a vote. This is the stable weight, not the one temporarily damped by a recent group — what you want for your own weighted roll.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.Danger
member.summary: How much the event can hurt the colony.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.ConditionMet
member.summary: Whether the event's condition passes right now. False means it would sit a vote out this moment — the "is it allowed" check you'd run before including it in a roll.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.WithinDangerCap
member.summary: Whether the event's danger is within the streamer's cap for the current cycle. Note ignores this cap, so respect it yourself if you don't want to fire past what the streamer allowed.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo.Eligible
member.summary: Whether the event would actually be offered in a vote drawn right now: weight above zero, condition met, and within the danger cap. The single flag most callers want.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi
member.summary:
Register your own events with Twitch Colony.
ILMerge this library into your mod's DLL (don't ship it loose — two mods shipping different versions of a loose TwitchColony.Api.dll would fight over which one loads).
Twitch Colony does not have to be installed. Everything here reflects into the main mod at call time and quietly does nothing if it isn't there, so your mod keeps working on its own — no hard reference, no TypeLoadException, nothing to catch.
public override void OnLoad(Harmony harmony) { base.OnLoad(harmony); TwitchColonyApi.RegisterEvent( id: "mymod.confetti", displayName: "Confetti!", action: ctx => Confetti.Drop(), danger: EventDanger.None, owner: "My Mod"); }

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.KeepZoom
member.summary: Zoom value meaning "don't touch the zoom, the streamer picked it".

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.CloseZoom
member.summary: Close enough to see one thing clearly. The default when you point at an object.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.IsAvailable
member.summary: True when Twitch Colony is installed and speaking a version we understand.

member.name: P:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.InstalledApiVersion
member.summary: Contract version of the installed Twitch Colony, or 0 if it isn't there.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.RegisterEvent(System.String,System.String,System.Action{System.Object},System.String,TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight,TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger,System.Func{System.Object,System.Boolean},System.String)
member.summary: Add an event to Twitch Colony's vote pool. Call it once, from your UserMod2.OnLoad; the event survives colony reloads.
member.param: Stable, unique id — prefix it with your mod name ("mymod.confetti"). If it collides with an id already registered, yours is refused.
What chat sees in the vote options.

Runs on the game's main thread when your event wins, so it may touch the game freely. The argument describes what triggered it: read it with (EventContext.GetStrings(ctx, EventContext.Voters) and friends). Never cast it to a type of your own — it's a plain Dictionary<string, object>.

Optional. Events that feel alike should share a group: when one fires, the whole group is less likely to be offered for the next few votes. Unlike , group names are a shared namespace: "flood" puts your events in the same bucket as Twitch Colony's floods (often what you want), while "mymod.floods" keeps them damping only each other.
How often it's offered relative to other events.
How much it can hurt the colony. Tag it honestly — streamers filter on this to keep a run from being ruined by something they didn't opt into.
Optional. Checked when the options are drawn; return false to sit this vote out. Keep it cheap and side-effect free. See for ready-made ones.
Your mod's name. Only used so log lines can name you.
member.returns: true if Twitch Colony accepted the event; false if it isn't installed, speaks a version we don't know, or rejected the arguments. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.TriggerEvent(System.String)
member.param: Id of a registered event — yours, or one of Twitch Colony's own.
member.returns: true if the event ran; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed or has no such id. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.TryGetEventData(System.String,TwitchColony.Api.EventDataInfo@)
member.param: Id of a registered event — yours, or one of Twitch Colony's own.
The event's state on success; null if there's no such id.
member.returns: true if the event exists; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed, is older than v2, or has no such id. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.ShowBanner(System.String,System.Single)
member.summary: Show a banner across the top of the screen — the same one Twitch Colony announces vote winners and new subs with. Use it to warn the streamer what just landed on them, or to deliver the punchline of an event. TwitchColonyApi.ShowBanner("Nobody expects the Spanish Inquisition!", 6f); TextMeshPro rich text works (, , newlines). Emoji don't: the game's fonts have none, so anything unrenderable is dropped rather than drawn as a hollow box. A second banner replaces the first. Main thread, colony loaded.
member.param: Keep it short — it's a banner, not a paragraph.
How long it stays up. Clamped to 1–30.
member.returns: true if it was shown; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.ShowBanner(System.String,System.Single,UnityEngine.GameObject,System.Single)
member.summary: A banner the streamer can click to look at whatever it's about — the camera pans to the object, keeping their zoom. It follows the object, so a moving one still works. TwitchColonyApi.ShowBanner("Something is loose in the base!", 8f, critter); Half the value of a warning is "…and it's over there", and the streamer shouldn't have to go hunting. The banner says "click to look" so they know it's there.
member.param: Keep it short — it's a banner, not a paragraph.
How long it stays up. Clamped to 1–30.
Where to take them. Nothing pans if it's null or gets destroyed.

Zoom to arrive at — smaller is closer, and it's clamped to what the game allows. Defaults to zooming in on the thing, since panning to a critter the streamer is zoomed out from just centres a dot. Pass to leave their zoom alone.
member.returns: true if it was shown; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.ShowBanner(System.String,System.Single,UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Single)
member.summary: Same, but for a fixed spot rather than an object — a cell you flooded, say.
member.param: Keep it short — it's a banner, not a paragraph.
How long it stays up. Clamped to 1–30.
The world position to pan to when clicked.
Zoom to arrive at. Defaults to — for a place rather than a thing, only you know how much of it the streamer needs to see.
member.returns: true if it was shown; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.ShowBubble(UnityEngine.GameObject,System.String)
member.summary:
Show a speech bubble above something — the same bubble viewers' chat messages appear in. Works over anything with a transform: a duplicant, a critter, a building.
TwitchColonyApi.ShowBubble(dupe.gameObject, "well, this is unexpected");
It follows the streamer's own bubble settings (font, size, lifetime), so it looks like part of the mod. One bubble per object — showing another replaces it. Emoji are dropped, as with banners.
Main thread, colony loaded.
member.param: What to float it above. Nothing happens if it's null or destroyed.
What it says.
member.returns: true if a bubble appeared; false if Twitch Colony isn't installed. Never throws.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.UnregisterEvent(System.String)
member.summary: Remove an event you registered earlier. Returns true if it was there.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.Resolve
member.summary: Find the main mod's bridge once and remember the answer, including "it isn't there". Matching the method by its exact parameter types is the real compatibility check: if a future Twitch Colony changes the signature, we find nothing and no-op rather than throwing a MissingMethodException into the add-on's face.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.TwitchColonyApi.ParametersOf(System.Type)
member.summary: The parameter types of a delegate, i.e. the signature to look for.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.RegisterEventDelegate
member.summary: The one true shape of the registration call. Both sides derive from this single declaration instead of spelling the signature out twice: the main mod assigns its method to it (so a changed parameter breaks the build, loudly, now) and the merge-lib builds its reflection lookup from it (so it can never ask for a signature that was never offered). Without this, the two would drift apart silently and add-on events would just stop registering with no error anywhere.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.UnregisterEventDelegate
member.summary: Same idea for the unregister call.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.TriggerEventDelegate
member.summary: And for firing an event on demand, which is how add-on authors test theirs.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.TryGetEventDataDelegate
member.summary: Read the current state of a registered event by id — its weight and danger, and whether it could be offered right now (its condition and the streamer's danger cap). The carrier is a Dictionary<string, object> (keys in ) for the same reason the action payload is: a struct of ours would have two runtime identities across the boundary. The merge-lib unpacks it into a typed EventDataInfo on its own side.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.ShowBannerDelegate
member.summary: Banner across the top of the screen. Note the UnityEngine type in the sibling delegate below: Unity's types are safe to pass across, unlike ours. Both mods reference the same UnityEngine.CoreModule out of the game folder, so there is exactly one GameObject type at runtime — the two-identities problem only bites on types we compile ourselves.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.ShowBannerAtTargetDelegate
member.summary: Banner that pans the camera to a moving thing when clicked. orthographicSize is the zoom to arrive at; 0 or less keeps whatever zoom the streamer is on.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.ShowBannerAtPositionDelegate
member.summary: Banner that pans the camera to a fixed spot when clicked. Same zoom rule.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.ShowBubbleDelegate
member.summary: Speech bubble above a game object.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.ApiContract
member.summary:
The exact names the merge-lib reflects for, and the version of the contract they follow.
This file is compiled into BOTH TwitchColony.dll and TwitchColony.Api.dll from the same source (see the Compile Include in each csproj) so the two can never drift apart. It is shared as source, not as a third DLL: a type compiled into two assemblies has two separate runtime identities, so nothing from here may ever be passed across the boundary. Only types both sides already share — string, int, bool, System.Action, System.Func, object — are allowed in the bridge signature.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.ApiContract.ModAssemblyName
member.summary: Assembly to look for. Missing = Twitch Colony isn't installed; the API no-ops.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.ApiContract.BridgeTypeName
member.summary: Full name of the static class in the main mod that the merge-lib reflects into.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.ApiContract.Version
member.summary:
Bumped only for a breaking change to the bridge signature. The merge-lib refuses to register (rather than throwing) against a main mod whose version it doesn't know, so an old add-on can't take the game down with a MissingMethodException.
v2 added . It's a pure addition — the register signature is unchanged, so a v1 add-on still resolves and runs against a v2 mod, and a v2 add-on falls back gracefully (TryGetEventData just returns false) against a v1 mod, exactly as TriggerEvent did when it was added. The number is the honest capability signal for add-ons that want to check InstalledApiVersion >= 2.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext
member.summary:
Reads the object handed to an event's action.
The payload is a plain Dictionary<string, object> — deliberately, not a nice context class. A class of ours compiled into two assemblies would be two unrelated types at runtime, so an add-on's cast would throw. A dictionary of BCL types always works, and new keys can be added later without breaking add-ons built against an older version.
Every getter tolerates a null or unexpected payload and returns the fallback: an event must never blow up because it was triggered from somewhere that didn't fill a key in.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.EventId
member.summary: Id of the event being triggered. string.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.Cycle
member.summary: Colony cycle when it fired, or -1 if the clock wasn't up yet. int.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.VoteCount
member.summary: Votes this option received. int. 0 when it wasn't a vote (see ).

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.Voters
member.summary: Nicks that voted for this option. string[]. Empty for native Twitch polls — Twitch reports totals, not who voted.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.Source
member.summary: What set it off: , , or other. string.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.SourceChatVote
member.summary: Value of when chat voted by typing the vote command.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.SourcePoll
member.summary: Value of when the winner came from a native Twitch poll.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.SourceDirect
member.summary: Value of when something fired the event directly, no vote involved.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.GetString(System.Object,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Reads a string key, or if it's missing or another type.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.GetInt(System.Object,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads a numeric key, or if it's missing or not a number.

member.name: M:TwitchColony.Api.EventContext.GetStrings(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Never returns null, so callers can foreach without checking.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger
member.summary:
How much an event can hurt the colony. Crossing the assembly boundary it travels as a plain int (see ).
The streamer decides how rough their run gets, so tag honestly: an event tagged that can kill duplicants will ruin somebody's colony on a setting they picked precisely to stop that happening.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger.None
member.summary: Cosmetic or pure upside. Nothing can go wrong: bubbles, cheering, free stuff.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger.Small
member.summary: Mildly annoying. Some work is lost or interrupted; nothing breaks.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger.Medium
member.summary: Costs real resources or time, and a fragile colony could feel it.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger.High
member.summary: Can damage the colony badly if the player doesn't react.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventDanger.Deadly
member.summary: Duplicants can die.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventData
member.summary:
The keys of the dictionary TryGetEventData hands back — the mod fills it, the merge-lib reads it into a typed EventDataInfo. Declared once here and compiled into both assemblies (like ) so the two can't disagree on a key name.
It travels as a plain Dictionary<string, object> for the usual reason: a type of ours would be two unrelated types across the boundary, and a new key can be added later without breaking an add-on built against an older version.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.Id
member.summary: The event's stable id. string.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.DisplayName
member.summary: What chat sees in the vote options. string.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.GroupId
member.summary: The event's group id, or null if it stands on its own. string.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.Weight
member.summary: Its configured draw weight (see EventWeight); 0 = never offered by a vote. int.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.Danger
member.summary: How much it can hurt the colony (see EventDanger). int.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.ConditionMet
member.summary: Whether its condition passes right now — false = it'd sit this vote out. bool.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.WithinDangerCap
member.summary: Whether its danger is within the streamer's current (cycle-scaled) cap. bool.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventData.Eligible
member.summary: Whether it would actually be offered in a vote drawn right now: weight above zero, its condition met, and within the danger cap. The one flag most callers want. bool.

member.name: T:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight
member.summary: How often an event should turn up in the vote options, relative to the others. Crossing the assembly boundary it travels as a plain int (see ).

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight.Never
member.summary: Registered, but never offered by the random draw. For events you trigger yourself.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight.Rare
member.summary: Offered a quarter as often as .

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight.Uncommon
member.summary: Offered half as often as .

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight.Common
member.summary: The default, and what every built-in event uses unless it says otherwise.

member.name: F:TwitchColony.Api.EventWeight.VeryCommon
member.summary: Offered twice as often as . Use sparingly.
